description: The world's most widely consumed stimulant, found in coffee, tea, and many soft drinks. It works by blocking adenosine receptors; at ordinary doses its kinetics are linear, making it a clean teaching example.

halfLife.conditions: healthy adults; label states T1/2 = 5 h once metabolism matures (~9 months of age). Neonatal half-life is far longer (~3–4 days) due to immature hepatic metabolism and is out of scope for the 70 kg adult reference subject.

vd.conditions: adults; mean volume of distribution 0.6 L/kg (vs 0.8–0.9 L/kg in infants); scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time

F.conditions: caffeine is nearly completely absorbed after oral intake (~100% bioavailability; negligible first-pass metabolism)

tmax.conditions: adult oral absorption is rapid; representative Tmax ≈ 1 h. Reported Tmax spans ~30 min–2 h with gastric-emptying variability — a curatorial point value within that range, not a single measured mean

metabolites.name: Paraxanthine (1,7-dimethylxanthine)
metabolites.description: The dominant, pharmacologically active metabolite of caffeine (~80% of its breakdown). Its shorter half-life makes it formation-rate-limited — it peaks lower and later than caffeine and fades with the parent.

fractionFormed.conditions: Lelo et al. 1986 (partial-clearances paper): the partial clearance of caffeine to paraxanthine is 79.6 +/- 21.0% of caffeine's TOTAL plasma clearance (and 83.9 +/- 5.4% of the demethylation pathways alone). Because caffeine is ~97% metabolized (<=3% excreted unchanged renally), fraction of total elimination ~= fraction of metabolism. BUT a partial-clearance fraction is a MOLAR fraction (fraction of caffeine MOLECULES taking that path), whereas the engine's fm multiplies parent MASS eliminated (dA_m/dt = fm.CL.C_p, all in mg, no MW factor). Each mg of caffeine (MW 194.19) cleared to paraxanthine yields only 180.16/194.19 = 0.928 mg paraxanthine (MW 180.16), so the mass-basis fm = 0.796 x 0.928 = 0.74. Stored value 74% (derived: true - the molar 79.6% mass-adjusted by the MW ratio, matching the allopurinol convention of feeding the engine a mass fraction, e.g. '90 mg oxypurinol per 100 mg allopurinol'). Range 65-78% is the ~70-84% molar band likewise mass-adjusted. The molar SD is large (individual CYP1A2 activity varies widely). See notes: paraxanthine is only ONE of THREE parallel demethylation products.

vd.conditions: Derived from Lelo 1986's directly-measured paraxanthine disposition (paraxanthine administered on its own): plasma clearance 2.20 mL/min/kg and half-life 3.1 h give Vd = CL.t½/ln2 = 0.132 L/h/kg / 0.2236 /h = 0.59 L/kg. This is an apparent Vd/F from oral paraxanthine; it is treated as a true metabolite volume because paraxanthine (a dimethylxanthine like caffeine) is near-completely absorbed, so F ~= 1 and Vd/F ~= Vd. Its own one-compartment volume, not the parent's.

halfLife.conditions: Lelo 1986: paraxanthine plasma half-life 3.1 h (six healthy males, paraxanthine dosed directly). This is SHORTER than the modelled caffeine half-life (5 h), so paraxanthine is FORMATION-rate-limited: its observed terminal decline tracks the slower parent (caffeine), not its own faster elimination, and it does NOT accumulate above the parent (contrast the elimination-rate-limited oxypurinol/nordiazepam). The engine's terminal-rate = min(k_parent, k_metabolite) reproduces this.

metabolites.notes:
Paraxanthine (1,7-dimethylxanthine) is the DOMINANT and pharmacologically ACTIVE metabolite of caffeine (adenosine-receptor antagonist and lipolytic, like the parent). It is computed as a Bateman curve off the one-compartment caffeine parent and drawn as a dashed line - the largest of the three parallel metabolite lines.

THE THREE-WAY SPLIT (all three drawn). Caffeine is demethylated by hepatic CYP1A2 into THREE parallel primary metabolites: paraxanthine (~80% of total clearance), theobromine (~11%), and theophylline (~4%), with the remaining ~15% going to 8-hydroxylation (1,3,7-trimethyluric acid) (Lelo 1986). The engine models any number of parallel metabolites - each an INDEPENDENT Bateman formed from the shared parent clearance (formation rate fm_i.CL.C_p) - so ALL THREE demethylation products are drawn as their own dashed lines, a faithful picture of caffeine's fate rather than a single dominant line. The three make a clean teaching set on formation- vs elimination-rate-limited kinetics: paraxanthine is FORMATION-limited (t½ 3.1 h < parent 5 h, so it peaks lower/earlier and its tail tracks the parent), whereas theobromine (t½ 7.2 h) and theophylline (t½ 6.2 h) are both ELIMINATION-limited (longer t½ than the parent, so they linger past it). The 8-hydroxylation product (trimethyluric acid) is not a demethylation metabolite and is not drawn. Cross-references, documented in each metabolite's own notes: theobromine ALSO ships as its own standalone compound (compounds/theobromine.json - the primary methylxanthine of chocolate), the same molecule entered from its dietary route; and theophylline ALSO ships as its own standalone (compounds/theophylline.json), but against the MICHAELIS-MENTEN engine, because its elimination is capacity-limited at therapeutic single-drug concentrations (10-15 mg/L) - whereas as a ~3-4% caffeine metabolite it peaks only ~0.1-0.2 mg/L, ~150-300x below its Km of 24.1 mg/L, where the saturable rate collapses to first-order and the linear line drawn HERE is that model's own low-concentration limit (see that file's notes).

SOURCE COHERENCE. The metabolite parameters come from Lelo et al. 1986, which measured caffeine AND all three demethylated metabolites in the SAME six healthy male volunteers (each compound dosed directly) - the one-population source. The parent caffeine disposition is kept from the FDA label (half-life 5 h, Vd 0.6 L/kg) as already shipped; Lelo's caffeine values (t½ 4.1 h, Vd ~0.73 L/kg) are compatible healthy-volunteer figures. Because paraxanthine is formation-rate-limited, its terminal tracks whichever caffeine half-life is modelled (4.1 or 5 h), so the small parent-source difference does not change the qualitative curve. Each metabolite AUC (fm.D / (k_m.Vd_m)) is independent of the parent's disposition entirely.

MAGNITUDE (built engine curves, mass-basis fm). After a single 200 mg oral caffeine dose the parent peaks 4.15 mg/L at 1 h; paraxanthine peaks 1.02 mg/L at 6 h - lower and later, the formation-limited signature - while the two minor elimination-limited products peak lower and even later (theobromine ~0.17 mg/L at 9 h, theophylline ~0.08 mg/L at 8 h, both decaying more slowly than the parent). With regular intake (100 mg every 4 h) paraxanthine builds substantially behind the parent (~1.9 mg/L against a ~4.9 mg/L parent over a 6-dose window); the ~1:1 caffeine/paraxanthine near-parity famous in habitual users is a chronic-steady-state phenomenon reached over days, beyond this short plotted course. Drawn on all routes (the metabolite gate is iv_bolus || oral || iv_infusion).

metabolites.name: Theobromine (3,7-dimethylxanthine)
metabolites.description: A minor caffeine metabolite (~10%) that is also the main stimulant of chocolate. Its longer half-life makes it elimination-rate-limited, so it lingers after caffeine has gone.

fractionFormed.conditions: Lelo et al. 1986 (partial-clearances paper): the partial clearance of caffeine to theobromine is 10.8% of caffeine's TOTAL plasma clearance (12.1% of the demethylation pathways). Mass-adjusted like paraxanthine: a partial-clearance fraction is MOLAR, but the engine's fm multiplies parent MASS eliminated, and theobromine (MW 180.16, a dimethylxanthine isomer) yields 180.16/194.19 = 0.928 mg per mg of caffeine (MW 194.19) cleared this way, so mass-fm = 0.108 x 0.928 = 0.100 = 10%. Range 7-12% is the molar band likewise mass-adjusted. This is the SECOND-largest of caffeine's three parallel demethylation products (~8-fold smaller partial clearance than paraxanthine).

vd.conditions: Derived from Lelo 1986's directly-measured theobromine disposition (theobromine dosed on its own): plasma clearance 1.20 mL/min/kg and half-life 7.2 h give Vd = CL.t½/ln2 = 0.072 L/h/kg / 0.09627 /h = 0.748 L/kg. Apparent Vd/F treated as a true metabolite volume (theobromine is well absorbed, F ~= 1). IDENTICAL to the standalone theobromine.json disposition - same molecule, same Lelo measurement - so the two entries never disagree.

halfLife.conditions: Lelo 1986: theobromine plasma half-life 7.2 h (six healthy males, theobromine dosed directly). LONGER than the modelled caffeine half-life (5 h), so theobromine is ELIMINATION-rate-limited: unlike formation-limited paraxanthine, its terminal decline follows its OWN slower elimination and it lingers past the parent. Identical to the standalone theobromine.json value.

metabolites.notes: Theobromine (3,7-dimethylxanthine) is the second-largest of caffeine's three parallel primary metabolites (~10% mass-fm) and, in its own right, the principal methylxanthine of chocolate. Drawn as a dashed line off the caffeine parent; pharmacologically active (a milder adenosine antagonist than caffeine). Because its half-life (7.2 h) exceeds the parent's (5 h) it is ELIMINATION-rate-limited - the teaching contrast to the formation-limited paraxanthine line on the same chart. The disposition (t½ 7.2 h, Vd 0.75 L/kg) is byte-identical to the standalone compounds/theobromine.json: the same molecule and the same Lelo 1986 measurement, approached from two directions (formed from caffeine here; eaten as cocoa there), so the two entries can never disagree. fm mass-converted from Lelo's 10.8% molar partial clearance (0.108 x 180.16/194.19 = 10%).

metabolites.name: Theophylline (1,3-dimethylxanthine)
metabolites.description: The smallest caffeine metabolite (~3–4%) and, separately, a bronchodilator drug. It is drawn here only because at these tiny metabolite concentrations its kinetics stay linear.

fractionFormed.conditions: Lelo et al. 1986 (partial-clearances paper): the partial clearance of caffeine to theophylline is 3.7% of caffeine's TOTAL plasma clearance (4.0% of the demethylation pathways). Mass-adjusted like the other two: theophylline (MW 180.16, a dimethylxanthine isomer) yields 180.16/194.19 = 0.928 mg per mg of caffeine cleared this way, so mass-fm = 0.037 x 0.928 = 0.0343 = 3.4%. Range 2.8-4.2% is the molar band mass-adjusted. This is the SMALLEST of caffeine's three parallel demethylation products (~23-fold smaller partial clearance than paraxanthine).

vd.conditions: Derived from Lelo 1986's directly-measured theophylline disposition (theophylline dosed on its own): plasma clearance 0.93 mL/min/kg and half-life 6.2 h give Vd = CL.t½/ln2 = 0.0558 L/h/kg / 0.1118 /h = 0.499 L/kg, consistent with theophylline's well-known ~0.5 L/kg. Apparent Vd/F treated as a true metabolite volume (theophylline is well absorbed, F ~= 1).

halfLife.conditions: Lelo 1986: theophylline plasma half-life 6.2 h (six healthy males, theophylline dosed directly). LONGER than the modelled caffeine half-life (5 h), so theophylline - like theobromine - is ELIMINATION-rate-limited. NOTE: theophylline's OWN elimination is capacity-limited (Michaelis-Menten) at THERAPEUTIC concentrations (10-15 mg/L), which is why its standalone (compounds/theophylline.json) is a Michaelis-Menten compound with no half-life at all; but as a ~3.4% caffeine metabolite it peaks ~0.1-0.2 mg/L, far below its Km, where this fixed first-order 6.2 h half-life is an accurate approximation. Cross-check against that file: its dilute-limit half-life, ln2.Km.Vd/Vmax, is 7.2 h - inside the 5-9 h range stored here, and ~15% above this measured 6.2 h, a between-source residual documented in its notes. Its Vd is byte-identical to the 0.5 L/kg stored here.

metabolites.notes: Theophylline (1,3-dimethylxanthine) is the smallest of caffeine's three parallel primary metabolites (~3.4% mass-fm) - the classic bronchodilator xanthine, here appearing as a minor caffeine metabolite. Drawn as a dashed line off the caffeine parent; pharmacologically active. Elimination-rate-limited (t½ 6.2 h > parent 5 h), lingering past the parent like theobromine. THE HONESTY POINT - the same molecule, two representations, and why that is not a contradiction. Theophylline's elimination saturates (Michaelis-Menten) at therapeutic single-drug concentrations, so it ships as a STANDALONE (compounds/theophylline.json) against the nonlinear engine, with no half-life at all. This linear line is not a rival claim about that: as a ~3.4% metabolite of a normal caffeine dose theophylline reaches only ~0.1-0.2 mg/L, ~150-300x below its Km of 24.1 mg/L, and at c << Km the saturable rate Vmax.c/(Km+c) collapses to (Vmax/Km).c - an ordinary first-order process. So this line IS the standalone's own low-concentration limit, the same tie the engine's 'Km >> C collapses onto models.ts' test pins. It must still NOT be read as endorsing a linear plot of a THERAPEUTIC theophylline dose, which is what the standalone exists to draw correctly. Held together deliberately: the Vd here (0.5 L/kg) is byte-identical to the standalone's, so the two files cannot disagree about theophylline's volume. Nothing in CI checks that - loader.test.ts derives each file's own routes and never compares the two. fm mass-converted from Lelo's 3.7% molar partial clearance (0.037 x 180.16/194.19 = 3.4%).

fda_label.title: Caffeine Citrate Injection / Oral Solution (Sagent Pharmaceuticals) — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology (adult disposition values)

statpearls.title: Evans J, Richards JR, Battisti AS. Caffeine. StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; updated 2024 May 29 (adult oral absorption)

lelo_pk.title: Lelo A, Birkett DJ, Robson RA, Miners JO. Comparative pharmacokinetics of caffeine and its primary demethylated metabolites paraxanthine, theobromine and theophylline in man. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 1986;22(2):177-182 (PMID 3756065). Six healthy male volunteers, each compound given orally on separate occasions: plasma clearances caffeine 2.07, paraxanthine 2.20, theobromine 1.20, theophylline 0.93 mL/min/kg; half-lives caffeine 4.1, paraxanthine 3.1, theobromine 7.2, theophylline 6.2 h.

lelo_clearances.title: Lelo A, Miners JO, Robson RA, Birkett DJ. Quantitative assessment of caffeine partial clearances in man. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 1986;22(2):183-186 (PMC1401107, PMID 3756066). Partial clearance of caffeine to paraxanthine is 79.6 +/- 21.0% of total plasma clearance (83.9 +/- 5.4% of demethylation), to theobromine 10.8% (12.1% of demethylation), to theophylline 3.7% (4.0% of demethylation); the caffeine->paraxanthine partial clearance is ~8-fold that to theobromine and ~23-fold that to theophylline.

metabolism: Caffeine is demethylated by the liver enzyme CYP1A2 into three active metabolites in parallel — paraxanthine (the dominant one, ~80% of the breakdown) and smaller amounts of theobromine and theophylline — all drawn here as separate curves off the shared parent. They form a neat teaching set: paraxanthine is formation-rate-limited (shorter half-life than caffeine, so it peaks lower and later and fades with the parent), while theobromine and theophylline are elimination-rate-limited (longer half-lives, so they linger after caffeine is gone).
notes: Curated for the 70 kg adult reference subject. The disposition (elimination half-life 5 h, Vd 0.6 L/kg) is taken from the FDA Structured Product Label for caffeine citrate (Sagent), which states the ADULT values explicitly while contrasting them with the very different neonatal PK (t½ ≈ 3–4 days, Vd 0.8–0.9 L/kg) that the product is actually indicated for (apnea of prematurity). Because this tool models a 70 kg adult, only the adult figures are used; the neonatal regime is out of scope. The label's absorption data (Cmax, Tmax 30 min–2 h) is measured in PRETERM NEONATES and its 'absolute bioavailability was not fully examined', so oral F and Tmax are instead sourced from StatPearls adult pharmacology: caffeine is nearly completely absorbed (~100%) with negligible first-pass, so F = 1; Tmax is set to a representative 1 h within the reported ~0.5–2 h adult spread (a curatorial point value, marked as such in conditions). ke is taken from the 5 h half-life. Caffeine is metabolised primarily by hepatic CYP1A2; at typical dietary/therapeutic intakes (roughly ≤ 250–400 mg) elimination is approximately first-order and doses superpose, so linear: true — the recurring-dose (e.g. a cup every few hours) teaching case is the point of this entry. At high doses caffeine metabolism saturates (Michaelis–Menten) and becomes nonlinear, a regime out of scope for this linear model, analogous to acetaminophen in overdose. IV routes (caffeine citrate injection is a real product) share the route-independent disposition; F = 1 by definition. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
